evals.prompt: I want to update ForecastingItem records directly in Apex to correct forecast amounts for a few reps. Can I just use a standard DML update statement?
expected_concepts: ForecastingItem is read-only
DML on ForecastingItem throws INSUFFICIENT_ACCESS_OR_READONLY
Use ForecastingOwnerAdjustment or ForecastingManagerAdjustment instead
Modify Opportunity fields to influence ForecastingItem values
evals.rubric: Response must clearly state ForecastingItem cannot be inserted or updated via DML, explain the correct alternatives (adjustments or Opportunity field updates), and not suggest any DML workaround on ForecastingItem.

evals.topic: ForecastAmount vs OwnerOnlyAmount vs AdjustedAmount — double-count risk
evals.prompt: I need to build a report showing total forecasted revenue across all reps in my org for Q3. I wrote a SOQL query that sums ForecastAmount across all ForecastingItem rows for the period. The number is way too high. What am I doing wrong?
expected_concepts: ForecastAmount includes rolled-up subordinate amounts
Summing ForecastAmount across hierarchy double-counts
Use OwnerOnlyAmount filtered to leaf-node users instead
Parent rows already include all children
evals.rubric: Response must explain the hierarchy double-counting problem, state that ForecastAmount already includes children, and direct the user to sum OwnerOnlyAmount at leaf nodes instead.

evals.prompt: We have two active ForecastingTypes — Revenue and Quantity. When I query ForecastingItem without filtering by ForecastingTypeId, I get doubled rows for the same user and period. Why?
expected_concepts: Each ForecastingType generates its own ForecastingItem tree
Must always filter ForecastingItem by ForecastingTypeId
Revenue and Quantity types produce separate rows for the same user and period
Mixing types without filtering produces incorrect aggregates
evals.rubric: Response must explain that each active ForecastingType produces its own independent set of ForecastingItem records and that ForecastingTypeId is a required filter for accurate queries.

evals.topic: ForecastingQuota matching logic — no lookup field
evals.prompt: I'm trying to write a report that shows each rep's quota next to their closed amount. I looked at ForecastingQuota and ForecastingItem but I can't find a ForecastingItemId field on ForecastingQuota. How do I join these two objects?
expected_concepts: ForecastingQuota has no lookup to ForecastingItem
Join on StartDate + ForecastingTypeId + QuotaOwnerId = OwnerId
Two-query approach in Apex
Joined report or external BI tool for UI reporting
evals.rubric: Response must confirm there is no ForecastingItemId field on ForecastingQuota, explain the three-field join key (StartDate, ForecastingTypeId, QuotaOwnerId/OwnerId), and provide a workable approach for building the join.

evals.prompt: We want overlay reps (solutions engineers) to see their credited share of deals in the Forecasts tab. Overlay splits don't need to sum to 100%. What objects and settings do I need to configure?
expected_concepts: Enable Opportunity Splits in Setup
Create OpportunitySplitType with IsTotalValidated = false for overlay
Overlay split percentages are additive — do not need to sum to 100%
Create an overlay ForecastingType linked to the overlay split type
Add OpportunitySplit records with SplitOwnerId for each overlay rep
evals.rubric: Response must cover both Opportunity Splits enablement AND the overlay ForecastingType configuration. Must note that overlay splits are additive (IsTotalValidated = false) and do not need to sum to 100%.

evals.topic: Territory vs role hierarchy forecast setting
evals.prompt: We use Enterprise Territory Management and want forecast rollups to follow our territory hierarchy rather than the role hierarchy. How do we configure this?
expected_concepts: ForecastingType.ForecastingHierarchyType = RoleTerritory2 for territory-based
Enterprise Territory Management must be enabled
Users must be assigned to Territory2 records via UserTerritory2Association
Separate ForecastingType needed for territory forecast vs role forecast
Territory2Model must be in Active state
evals.rubric: Response must specify ForecastingHierarchyType = RoleTerritory2, note the Enterprise Territory Management prerequisite, and explain that user-to-territory assignment via UserTerritory2Association is required.

evals.prompt: A manager submitted their Q3 forecast last week. Since then, two deals closed. The submitted forecast still shows lower numbers. Is this a bug, and how should I advise the manager?
expected_concepts: ForecastingSubmission is a point-in-time snapshot
Opportunity changes after submission do not update the submitted forecast
Live ForecastingItem updates reflect the closed deals
Manager can create a new ForecastingSubmission to capture the latest snapshot
This is expected behavior, not a bug
evals.rubric: Response must explain that ForecastingSubmission is a static snapshot, that post-submission opportunity changes only affect live ForecastingItem data, and that a new submission is needed to capture the updated numbers.

evals.topic: Product family ForecastingType requires OpportunityLineItem records
evals.prompt: We enabled a Product Family forecast type and most of our reps have $0 in their product family forecast, even though they have open opportunities. What's wrong?
expected_concepts: Product family forecast excludes opportunities without OpportunityLineItem records
Only opportunities with products (OLIs) appear in product family forecast
Opportunities without line items are silently excluded
Products must have Product2.Family populated
evals.rubric: Response must identify that opportunities without OpportunityLineItem records are excluded from product family forecasts entirely, and direct the user to check whether their opportunities have products added.

evals.prompt: We want to build a forecast that rolls up our custom ARR__c field on Opportunity instead of the standard Amount field. Is this supported, and how do we set it up?
expected_concepts: Custom ForecastingType can roll up a custom Opportunity field
Field must be Currency or Number type
Set ForecastedAmountFieldApiName to ARR__c
Rollup recalculation is asynchronous — up to 24 hours
Configure via Setup → Forecast Settings → Forecast Types
evals.rubric: Response must confirm this is supported, specify the field type requirement (Currency or Number), explain how ForecastedAmountFieldApiName is configured, and note the asynchronous recalculation delay.

evals.prompt: Our sales ops team wants to switch our Revenue forecast type from Monthly to Quarterly. What risks should I communicate before they make this change?
expected_concepts: Changing period type deletes all monthly ForecastingItem records
All ForecastingOwnerAdjustment and ForecastingManagerAdjustment records for monthly periods are deleted
Deletion is not reversible
New quarterly records are regenerated from Opportunity data
Export adjustment data before making the change
evals.rubric: Response must clearly call out that the period type change is destructive and irreversible, that all existing adjustment records for the old period granularity are deleted, and recommend backing up adjustment data first.

evals.prompt: A manager wants to reduce one of their rep's Commit number in the forecast because a deal is going to slip. Which object should the manager use to make this adjustment, and can they use ForecastingOwnerAdjustment on behalf of the rep?
expected_concepts: Manager must use ForecastingManagerAdjustment — not ForecastingOwnerAdjustment
ForecastingOwnerAdjustment can only be created by the opportunity owner on their own row
ForecastingManagerAdjustment.OwnerId = manager's User ID
ForecastingManagerAdjustment.ForecastingItemId = the rep's ForecastingItem row
evals.rubric: Response must clearly distinguish the two objects, state that the manager must use ForecastingManagerAdjustment, and explicitly confirm that a manager cannot create ForecastingOwnerAdjustment on behalf of a report.

evals.topic: Cumulative forecast view vs standard view
evals.prompt: The Forecasts tab has a 'Cumulative' toggle. Does toggling it change the underlying ForecastingItem values? And if I want to report cumulative YTD closed revenue in SOQL, is there a single field I can query?
expected_concepts: Cumulative toggle only changes the UI display — does not change ForecastingItem values
No single field stores YTD or cumulative amounts on ForecastingItem
Must SUM OwnerOnlyAmount across multiple StartDate values in SOQL
ForecastingDisplayedFamily also affects UI display only
evals.rubric: Response must clarify that the cumulative toggle is a UI-only feature, confirm there is no stored cumulative field, and provide or describe a SOQL approach using SUM(OwnerOnlyAmount) across a date range.
